summary: Electric power is the rate at which a device converts electrical energy: P = U·I. It tells you how bright a bulb is or how fast a heater warms a room.
method: Identify the device and the voltage across it and current through it.
Decide which quantity is unknown — power, voltage, or current.
Rearrange P = U·I for the unknown.
If you know the resistance instead, combine with Ohm's law: P = I²·R or P = U²/R.
Use SI units (V, A, W) and substitute.

example.title: Power drawn by a light bulb
steps: The unknown is P, so use P = U·I.
